reasoning: Credit card loans generally exhibit strong secular upward trends in nominal terms, primarily driven by inflation and economic expansion. Although slight economic cooling might slow the growth rate, actual deleveraging (a nominal decrease) typically only occurs during severe recessions or major credit contractions. Since the data is seasonally adjusted, we also don't expect routine post-holiday drops in early 2026 (Jan/Feb) to negatively affect the compared values. Thus, over the approx. 3-month period from Oct 2025 to Jan 2026, an increase is highly probable.
